segments.text: Jason: Good morning, Smart Travel, Jason speaking.
Woman: Hi, good morning.
Woman: I saw your advert in today's paper about hot air balloon trips.
Woman: It sounds pretty exciting, and I wonder how much it costs.
Jason: Well, the price may vary depending on the location, number of participants and add-ons.
Jason: A single ride may cost you up to 280 pounds per person.
Jason: But if you choose group booking, there is a discount.
Jason: And the cheapest flight is 125 pounds each for a group booking of at least ten people.
Woman: Oh, that's not too bad.
Woman: What do you mean by add-ons?
Jason: They are optional items like taking photos for you during the ride, champagne and sit-down breakfast after the flight.
Jason: Upon completing the trip, we will provide each participant with a certificate.
Jason: And that is entirely free of charge.
Woman: Can I book a ride over the phone?
Jason: Yes, that's an option.
Jason: Reservations can be made one year ahead of your trip by telephone.
Jason: But to pick a specific date, you must book online.
Jason: A confirmation email will be sent to you right afterwards.
Woman: How high can the balloons go?
Jason: A hot air balloon's height is limited by the temperature of the air and how much fuel it can carry.
Jason: And the highest altitude ever recorded for a ride is 1800 metres.
Jason: But most rides stay below 300 metres.
Woman: Oh boy.
Woman: That's impressive.
Woman: How long is a single ride?
Jason: The average hot air balloon ride lasts about an hour.
Jason: The entire ballooning experience, which includes ground preparation, flight and post-flight packing up and celebration, may take two to three hours.
Woman: And what time are the flights?
Jason: Our flights generally meet about an hour before sunrise on the day.
Jason: You will be notified of the exact meeting time the evening before your flight.
Jason: Night flights are avoided due to weather conditions.
Woman: Fair enough.
Woman: Which month do you recommend for a hot air balloon trip?
Jason: Autumn is the best time of year to take the trip.
Jason: Not only can you see the changing hues of the trees from above, but it is a great way to spend time with the whole family before it gets too cold.
Woman: I see.
Jason: But please note that our flight may be cancelled, and you will be notified by phone the day before.
Woman: Why is that?
Jason: It can be quite dangerous to fly under bad weather conditions like a thunderstorm.
Jason: So our experienced pilots will not go under such conditions.
Woman: What should I wear for the ride?
Jason: We suggest wearing comfortable clothing like a jacket.
Jason: Multiple layers are always better.
Jason: Wearing a helmet for safety concerns is also a good idea.
Jason: Sandals are not permitted.
Woman: Can children or older people participate?
Jason: All children must be above the minimum age of 9 years old.
Jason: And they have to be accompanied by at least a parent or guardian.
Jason: However, an adult has no age limit as long as they meet the health requirements.
Woman: What exactly are the health requirements?
Jason: Participants must at least be in fair physical condition.
Jason: For example, they shall have no significant knee, hip or back problems and no recent surgeries or broken bones.
Jason: They must also stand unassisted for an hour during the flight.
Jason: It is the required position during the whole flight.
Jason: As the balloon comes to land, all passengers will need to adopt the landing position with knees bent in front of them.
Woman: Are those rides safe?
Jason: Hot air balloon companies are required to have a Commercial Balloon Pilot licence, and all our pilots go through rigorous training before they are allowed to fly.
Jason: It is a relatively gentle activity.
Jason: However, the impact of a balloon landing can vary drastically depending on factors like wind speed.
Jason: Sometimes it is similar to the momentum of jumping off a small chair.
Jason: If you cannot jump off a small chair, speaking to your doctor before booking a balloon flight is best.
Woman: Thank you very much.
Woman: I think that's all I need to know.
Jason: Thanks for calling.
Jason: Goodbye.
